content:
To figure out where each excelled and where it failed

14 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Just for fun — there is an actual graph further down | Images by authorThere is a huge market for Intelligent Document Processing (IDP), and it is projected to grow to somewhere between $20 billion and $90 billion by the early 2030s, depending on which analyst you ask.
Probably driven by companies paying $15–25 per invoice in manual handling costs.
Because I stay close to the tech world, I have watched a wave of small, specialized OCR vision models ship over the past year, mostly from Chinese organizations.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

These are not the only new options, though. Document-parsing tools such as LlamaParse and Docling have also come onto the scene.
These new document-parsing models and pipelines now share the space with old-timers such as Tesseract and AWS Textract Structured. Depending on what they are being used for, the price tag for companies can currently range from $0 to around $65 per 1,000 processed pages.
